feedback_addressed: Added 3 SELL recommendations (INTC, PLTR AVOID, DELL) with detailed rationale and FCF analysis
Recalibrated NVDA moat from 10 to 8 acknowledging AMD MI450, Google TPU, Broadcom XPU competitive threats
Downgraded PLTR from BUY to HOLD with 112x forward PE properly flagged as extreme valuation risk
Added comprehensive position sizing with stop-losses for every pick (18% NVDA, 15% AVGO, 12% GOOGL, etc.)
Added scenario analysis (base/bull/bear with probability weights) for all 8 picks
Fixed scoring formula math with explicit verification for every pick including qualitative adjustment notes
Added reverse DCF analysis for NVDA showing market implies 25-30% revenue CAGR for 5 years
Added peer comparison tables for semiconductor and enterprise software peers with PEG, FCF margin, FCF yield
Added correlation analysis identifying 27% semiconductor cluster exposure with effective 3-4 independent bets
Added historical cycle analog (dot-com fiber buildout, 2018 cloud overinvestment) with comparison metrics
Added FCF analysis for every pick including yield, margin, and interest coverage
Added NVDA customer concentration analysis (61% top 4, up from 36%)
Added insider activity note for NVDA
Added semiconductor cycle positioning (near peak per 16-quarter model)
remaining_flaws: NVDA score adjusted from formula output of 8.1 to 7.9 with qualitative explanation, but qualitative adjustments should be more systematically applied across all picks or none
TSM score rounded up from 8.9 to 9.0 without strong justification -- minor but inconsistent with the downward adjustments elsewhere
PLTR scenario analysis base case shows -12% return, yet rating is HOLD not SELL -- the line between HOLD and SELL for existing positions could be more precisely defined
Cash reserve of 56% is aggressive and could be seen as a timing call rather than fundamental analysis -- the framework for deploying that cash needs more structure
what_impressed: The dot-com capex/GDP ratio comparison (4.4% current vs 4.5% peak) is exactly the kind of historical context that separates professional research from amateur analysis
Correlation analysis showing 27% semiconductor cluster is effectively one trade is genuinely useful portfolio construction insight
The PLTR downgrade from BUY to HOLD with detailed ServiceNow comparison demonstrates intellectual honesty -- the analyst reversed a bad call with evidence
FCF analysis across all picks with interest coverage ratios and yield comparisons provides the financial rigor that was completely absent in v1
INTC SELL recommendation with negative FCF, declining revenue, and 33% overvaluation vs Morningstar fair value is a well-reasoned contrarian call
The forward curve vs spot price distinction for semiconductor cycle timing shows maturity in cycle analysis
